hero.title: Designing products with clarity, character, and longevity.
hero.body: I'm Jon Brooks, a product designer with a strong interest in furniture, creating considered products with clear function, honest materials, and details built to age well.

workIntro: A focused selection of product and furniture studies balancing softened forms, durable joinery, and materials that invite touch.

projects.title: Contour Lounge Chair
projects.materials: Dark olive upholstery, sculpted walnut frame, recessed support rails
projects.summary: A low lounge chair study with a broad upholstered seat held inside a continuous timber frame. The side profile is the main gesture: quiet, reclined, and shaped to feel substantial without looking heavy.
notes: Swept rear frame creates a clear silhouette from the side.
Dark upholstery lets the cushion read as one calm volume.
Low seat angle gives the chair a relaxed lounge posture.

projects.title: Silhouette Sofa
projects.materials: Olive boucle upholstery, hardwood frame, blackened stone table
projects.summary: A low, rounded sofa study designed around a single continuous gesture. The back, arms, and seat read as one softened volume while the foreground table gives the piece a grounded architectural counterpoint.
notes: Continuous wraparound back for a calm profile from every angle.
Dense upholstery texture chosen to hold shadow and soften wear.
Low table pairing reinforces the sofa's horizontal silhouette.

projects.title: Arc Lounge Chair
projects.materials: Cream boucle, sculpted walnut, concealed webbing
projects.summary: A compact chair built around a continuous walnut arm rail. The cream upholstered forms sit inside the frame rather than on top of it, making the construction feel light without becoming fragile.
notes: Rounded walnut transitions remove hard stops at the arm and front leg.
Generous cushion radius gives the chair a softer domestic posture.
Contrasting upholstery and timber make the frame legible at a glance.

projects.title: Foundry Sideboard
projects.materials: Ribbed walnut, satin finish, slim timber legs
projects.summary: A long storage piece with a precise ribbed front and restrained styling surface. The rhythm of the vertical fluting adds depth while keeping the cabinet quiet enough for living spaces.
notes: Vertical ribbing breaks up the broad face without visual noise.
Inset legs keep the cabinet mass floating just above the floor.
Satin walnut finish holds detail in low light and avoids glare.

projects.title: Ridge Dining Table
projects.materials: Dark walnut, satin finish, twin cove-cut pedestal bases, rounded bullnose top
projects.summary: A dining table study built around a softened rounded-rectangle top and two arch-cut pedestal bases. Every view uses the same walnut table design: a thick bullnose edge, block bases, and repeated arched openings.
notes: Rounded ends and a beveled underside soften the long table volume.
Twin pedestal bases create a stable stance without crowding the corners.
Satin walnut finish keeps the grain visible in low, warm interior light.

about.title: About
about.body: Jon Brooks is a product designer with a strong interest in furniture, broad experience across design development, AI-assisted image generation, cross-platform coding, and custom tools. He has ventured into learning and trying as many AI tools as he can get his hands on, using them to test ideas faster and make each design direction more resolved. He is naturally investigative: when a subject is unfamiliar, he makes it his mission to learn it deeply, test different methods, and turn new ideas into practical design capability. He values that everyone has their own way of working, and stays open to new methods, perspectives, and workflows rather than defaulting to one fixed approach.
about.experienceIntro: Overall experience that supports product design work, from furniture and physical product thinking through AI visual workflows, coding, custom app development, and practical design problem-solving.

experience.description: Developing furniture and broader product concepts with a focus on proportion, material character, presentation imagery, and build-ready design intent.

Extensive experience using AI and image generation while actively learning and trying as many AI tools as possible to explore form, material mood, lighting, room context, and high-end presentation directions before committing to a final design path.
Building custom apps and tools across multiple platforms to support design work, speed up repeated tasks, organize ideas, and connect visual exploration with practical project decisions.
Combining hands-on design judgment with outside-the-box thinking, technical curiosity, and a willingness to investigate unfamiliar subjects until they become usable design capability.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: For commissions, collaborations, and project inquiries, send a note with the scope, timeline, room context, and materials you have in mind.
